exploratory_general.label: General exploratory research
exploratory_general.description: Fallback profile for topic work that does not yet fit a tighter numerical, toy-model, or formal lane.
reproducibility_expectations: Persist all claimed intermediate artifacts under the topic run directory.
Keep the task/result contract honest about what actually ran and what remained manual.
Record the next bounded follow-up step before escalating a claim.
note_expectations: L2 note: promote only reusable conclusions with explicit scope.
L3 note: keep a human-readable exploratory note explaining the active uncertainty.
L4 note: keep a validation summary that separates evidence from non-conclusions.

first_principles.label: First-principles / numerical
first_principles.description: For ab initio, GW/QSGW, exact-diagonalization-with-fixtures, or other backend-heavy validations that depend on configs, baselines, and reproducible outputs.
reproducibility_expectations: Persist exact configs, input decks, and environment assumptions with the run.
Keep baseline references and validation criteria auditable before treating a backend result as trusted.
Write summaries that name numerical limitations, convergence gaps, and missing controls explicitly.
note_expectations: L2 note: state the reusable numerical pattern, trusted regime, and baseline gate.
L3 note: document planned parameter ranges, observables, and backend assumptions.
L4 note: summarize what was run, what artifacts were produced, and what remains unvalidated.

toy_model.label: Spin-chain / toy-model
toy_model.description: For reduced models, spin chains, lattice toy problems, and rapid-but-auditable exploratory calculations where tractability matters more than large-scale infrastructure.
reproducibility_expectations: Persist the exact model definition, symmetry sector, and parameter choices used for each result.
Keep toy-model conclusions explicitly scoped so they are not over-transferred to the full theory.
Record small-size and observable-choice limitations in the durable result summary.
note_expectations: L2 note: state which toy-model mechanism is believed reusable and where it stops applying.
L3 note: explain the active toy model, symmetry sector, and why it is the right reduction.
L4 note: write a limitation-aware validation note that distinguishes mechanism evidence from final theory claims.

formal_derivation.label: Formal derivation
formal_derivation.description: For proof-like, algebraic, bootstrap, or analytic derivation work where the key outputs are assumptions, logical dependencies, and human-checkable derivation notes.
reproducibility_expectations: Persist the assumption set, notation choices, and dependency chain used by the derivation.
Separate proved statements, conjectural steps, and heuristic intuition in the durable artifacts.
Record the exact obstruction when a derivation fails or remains incomplete.
note_expectations: L2 note: promote only derivation fragments that are reusable with explicit assumptions.
L3 note: maintain a human-readable derivation notebook with named lemmas or checkpoints.
L4 note: summarize what was established, what was only sketched, and what requires further proof.
